title: Heterogeneous GPU headroom miscount causing silent dedicated shortfalls
preview: Multiple enterprise customers observed silent capacity shortfalls in Reserved Pools which manifested as increased cold-starts and higher p95 latency during burst traffic windows. Initial triage showed autoscaler did not add headroom quickly enough for tenants using GPU variants with lower vCPU equivalence, causing t...
text: Multiple enterprise customers observed silent capacity shortfalls in Reserved Pools which manifested as increased cold-starts and higher p95 latency during burst traffic windows. Initial triage showed autoscaler did not add headroom quickly enough for tenants using GPU variants with lower vCPU equivalence, causing the scheduler to under-allocate across heterogeneous pools. This ticket tracks the investigation, immediate mitigations, hotfix rollout, and follow-up reliability work.

title: Tenant prefetch circuit breaker and jittered scale controls
preview: Follow-up action item from recent hosted API GPU exhaustion incident where tenant prefetch and aggressive priming caused rapid queue buildup and cascading throttles. This ticket tracks design, implementation, testing, and rollout of a multi-pronged mitigation: a tenant-level prefetch circuit breaker, request admissi...
text: Follow-up action item from recent hosted API GPU exhaustion incident where tenant prefetch and aggressive priming caused rapid queue buildup and cascading throttles. This ticket tracks design, implementation, testing, and rollout of a multi-pronged mitigation: a tenant-level prefetch circuit breaker, request admission safeguards that cap prefetch concurrency, and jittered scale-up to avoid synchronized priming across multiple tenants. Goal: prevent multi-tenant cascade events that saturate GPU pools while preserving tail latency for interactive customers.

title: Warp-tiler slab-wrap and prefetch index-fence leading to KV overwrite and subtle numeric skew
preview: Summary: A concurrency bug in the warp-tiler prefetch path can cause an off-by-one slab wrap combined with an index-fence ordering violation. That results in KV slab slots being overwritten during concurrent prefill+decode windows. Symptoms include rare token duplication, subtle numerical drift in softmax accumulato...
text:
Summary: A concurrency bug in the warp-tiler prefetch path can cause an off-by-one slab wrap combined with an index-fence ordering violation. That results in KV slab slots being overwritten during concurrent prefill+decode windows. Symptoms include rare token duplication, subtle numerical drift in softmax accumulators under mixed-precision, and occasional transient degradation in prompt continuation quality.

Steps to reproduce (intermittent):
1) Start a dedicated workload with adaptive-kernel-routing enabled (--adaptive-kernel=true) on an 8-GPU pool and model variant 'redwood-open-16k-quant'.
2) Send a burst of interleaved prefill requests (batch_size=4, prefix lengths varying 512-1024) while simultaneously issuing low-latency decode continuations on other shards.
3) Observe error rate increase and rerun with debug KV instrumentation enabled (RUNTIME_KV_TRACE=1) to capture slab indic...

title: Adaptive batcher hang analysis and mitigations
preview: Summary: On 2026-03-09 at ~03:18 UTC we observed a multi-hour regression where the adaptive continuous batcher entered a state where new batches failed to form for particular traffic mixes, causing 95th/99th token latency to spike and sustained throughput degradation for affected tenants. This ticket captures the in...
text:
Summary: On 2026-03-09 at ~03:18 UTC we observed a multi-hour regression where the adaptive continuous batcher entered a state where new batches failed to form for particular traffic mixes, causing 95th/99th token latency to spike and sustained throughput degradation for affected tenants. This ticket captures the incident analysis, short-term mitigations we pushed, and the longer-term action plan to prevent recurrence.

What happened: High proportion of long-sequence, low-priority requests mixed with a burst of short, high-priority requests exposed a race condition in the batch formation state machine. The adaptive timer that delays batch finalization to improve throughput was repeatedly re-armed under a specific admission/eviction interplay. Combined with a KV-cache hotlock and worker recycling during a burst of autoscaler activity, many requests were repeatedly queued but never comm...

title: Sequenced Release Safety Thermostat and Automated Rollback Orchestra
preview: Design and implement a 'thermostat' control loop for sequenced progressive delivery that dynamically adjusts percentage ramps, enforces multi-signal gates (service SLOs, error budget burn, anomaly score, infra utilization), and coordinates an automated rollback orchestra which executes safe, observable reversion ste...
text: Design and implement a 'thermostat' control loop for sequenced progressive delivery that dynamically adjusts percentage ramps, enforces multi-signal gates (service SLOs, error budget burn, anomaly score, infra utilization), and coordinates an automated rollback orchestra which executes safe, observable reversion steps across model variants, feature flags, and routing layers. This ticket covers design, simulation, a staging run, and an initial production pilot for Redwood's hosted and dedicated deployments.

title: KV cache placement and compaction heuristics for sustained throughput
preview: Problem: As sustained continuous-batching workloads run on long-lived sessions (chat, multi-turn generation), KV cache entries show two failure modes: (1) small-variable sized segments cause internal fragmentation on GPU allocator leading to increased OOM risk under peak load; (2) uneven placement of KV slabs across...
text: Problem: As sustained continuous-batching workloads run on long-lived sessions (chat, multi-turn generation), KV cache entries show two failure modes: (1) small-variable sized segments cause internal fragmentation on GPU allocator leading to increased OOM risk under peak load; (2) uneven placement of KV slabs across GPUs increases cross-GPU memory pressure and reduces available headroom for batching spikes. Goal: Design and implement a lightweight placement + compaction heuristic that minimizes fragmentation and keeps headroom for high-concurrency bursts without adding significant CPU overhead or serialization pauses. Requirements: compatible with existing KV slab allocator and defrag tooling, work with dynamic sequence length distributions, preserve KV cache correctness across model warm-starts and rollouts, and support online metrics for fragmentation and compaction effectiveness.

title: Kernel pulse signature and span anchoring for prefill/decode telemetry
preview: Problem: We lack a low-noise, high-fidelity signal that ties per-kernel GPU execution to high-level request phases (prefill vs decode) and scheduler decisions. Existing NVTX ranges and execid spans surface latency spikes, but we cannot reliably detect microburst kernel-path divergence caused by attention-path heuris...
text:
Problem: We lack a low-noise, high-fidelity signal that ties per-kernel GPU execution to high-level request phases (prefill vs decode) and scheduler decisions. Existing NVTX ranges and execid spans surface latency spikes, but we cannot reliably detect microburst kernel-path divergence caused by attention-path heuristics or KV-cache reuse without heavy sampling.

Proposal: Introduce a lightweight 'kernel pulse' marker emitted by the runtime around each kernel submission that contains a compact signature (string tags) describing the execution context. Anchor those pulses to trace spans so that backends (Tempo/Honeycomb) can reconstruct the full per-request kernel timeline without requiring full-trace capture every time. Pulse fields: kernel_name, kernel_variant, seq_len_bucket, kv_cache_hit, quant_path, stream_id, scheduler_decision, exec_id_short, bytes_read, bytes_written, timestamp_u...

title: Throughput certification runbook for Dedicated capacity (benchmark harness & acceptance tests)
preview: Create an operational runbook and test artifacts to certify Dedicated capacity SLAs and throughput guarantees. The goal is a repeatable, customer-facing acceptance test suite and an internal benchmark harness that measures sustained and burst throughput, autoscaling behavior, and graceful degradation across model va...
text: Create an operational runbook and test artifacts to certify Dedicated capacity SLAs and throughput guarantees. The goal is a repeatable, customer-facing acceptance test suite and an internal benchmark harness that measures sustained and burst throughput, autoscaling behavior, and graceful degradation across model variants and instance types. This ticket covers the benchmarking methodology, harness implementation notes, accepted measurement tools, and concrete acceptance criteria for customer handoff.

title: Canonicalize model variant identifiers and publish performance profile guides
preview: Problem: Newly ingested models and third-party variants arrive with inconsistent ID formats (vendor-flavor, model-vX, quantized tags, internal hash). This causes: confusion in pinning workflows, duplicate entries in the catalog, and errors when publishing performance profiles to the console. Goal: define a canonical...
text: Problem: Newly ingested models and third-party variants arrive with inconsistent ID formats (vendor-flavor, model-vX, quantized tags, internal hash). This causes: confusion in pinning workflows, duplicate entries in the catalog, and errors when publishing performance profiles to the console. Goal: define a canonical variant identifier scheme, implement a lightweight normalizer in the onboarding pipeline, and update docs so engineers and integrators can reliably pin specific variants and read precise performance profiles. Scope: - canonical ID spec (human-readable + stable fingerprint) - onboarding normalizer + lint step - profile publish workflow that records provenance + runtime signature - docs and console help text updates - migration strategy for existing catalog entries

title: Call Interoperability Liaison Bridge: reconcile OpenAI-style tools with legacy function surface
preview: Goal: Implement a lightweight, production-safe compatibility layer (Call Interoperability Liaison, CIL) that lets Redwood route and execute both OpenAI-style "tools" and our legacy "function" call surface with minimal client changes. The CIL will: 1) normalize names and parameter shapes, 2) preserve invocation prove...
text:
Goal: Implement a lightweight, production-safe compatibility layer (Call Interoperability Liaison, CIL) that lets Redwood route and execute both OpenAI-style "tools" and our legacy "function" call surface with minimal client changes. The CIL will: 1) normalize names and parameter shapes, 2) preserve invocation provenance and deterministic fallback behavior, 3) support streaming responses and partial tool/function returns, and 4) emit telemetry to detect regressions during migration.

Background: We have multiple customers that rely on older function-style integrations while a growing number adopt the OpenAI 'tool' contract. Current runtime has ad-hoc mappings which cause name collisions, lost params, and inconsistent streaming semantics. This ticket scopes the runtime bridge, a stable compatibility header, a per-customer alias registry, and a rollout plan.

Non-goals: This ticket does...

title: Adaptive onboarding checklist and pinning policy for experimental quantized models
preview: Establish an adaptive metadata schema, onboarding checklist, and a conservative pinning policy for newly-ingested experimental quantized variants (Q4/Q8/Q5) so that performance profiles are published reliably and runtime fallbacks behave predictably. This ticket covers schema additions, validation tooling, documenta...
text: Establish an adaptive metadata schema, onboarding checklist, and a conservative pinning policy for newly-ingested experimental quantized variants (Q4/Q8/Q5) so that performance profiles are published reliably and runtime fallbacks behave predictably. This ticket covers schema additions, validation tooling, documentation updates for the catalog, and a short-term rollout policy that balances dev velocity with safety for production customers.

title: Dynamic slew-rate limiter with progressive KV compaction to mitigate queue pileup at sustained high QPS
preview: Background: During recent weekend load tests and two customer incidents we observed sustained high QPS (50k+ req/s across the cluster) producing prolonged microbatch queue growth, increased tail latency, and KV cache pressure that eventually triggers out-of-memory (OOM) evictions and degraded throughput. Existing ad...
text:
Background: During recent weekend load tests and two customer incidents we observed sustained high QPS (50k+ req/s across the cluster) producing prolonged microbatch queue growth, increased tail latency, and KV cache pressure that eventually triggers out-of-memory (OOM) evictions and degraded throughput. Existing admission heuristics (static rate caps + simple headroom checks) react too late or oscillate under nonstationary traffic patterns.

Goal: Implement a coordinated admission and memory-reclamation strategy that prevents queue pileups before OOMs occur while preserving throughput and KV hit rate. Specifically: introduce a 'slew-rate limiter' to throttle the entrance of new microbatches smoothly and a progressive KV compaction policy to reclaim low-value cache entries when memory headroom falls below safe thresholds. The two mechanisms should be tenant-aware and integrate with ex...

title: Workload Signal Assembler & Continual Batching Advisor
preview: Build a recommendation engine that synthesizes workload signals (request size distribution, repeat prefixes, KV cache hit patterns, latency tail behavior) and outputs conservative continuous-batching parameters plus a safe apply/rollback plan. This ticket focuses on the signal assembler, heuristics for safe step siz...
text: Build a recommendation engine that synthesizes workload signals (request size distribution, repeat prefixes, KV cache hit patterns, latency tail behavior) and outputs conservative continuous-batching parameters plus a safe apply/rollback plan. This ticket focuses on the signal assembler, heuristics for safe step sizes, and the shadow-apply telemetry needed for automatic rollback triggers.

title: Central policy proxy and audit pipeline for RBAC v2
preview:
Summary:
Build a central policy evaluation proxy and an immutable audit pipeline to support RBAC v2 across the API surface (hosted API), the serving runtime, and the Redwood Console. This ticket covers the core engine (fast, deterministic policy evaluation), the authorization middleware to call it from different run...
text:
Summary:
Build a central policy evaluation proxy and an immutable audit pipeline to support RBAC v2 across the API surface (hosted API), the serving runtime, and the Redwood Console. This ticket covers the core engine (fast, deterministic policy evaluation), the authorization middleware to call it from different runtimes, audit event schema and transport, SSO/SAML role-to-policy mapping hooks, and the admin controls for policy editing and rollout.

Goals:
- Implement a lightweight, horizontally scalable policy evaluation service (gRPC + HTTP sidecar) capable of evaluating token and context attributes within 1ms P50 for typical requests.
- Provide language-agnostic middleware libraries for the API gateway, runtime request pipeline, and console backend so all requests go through a consistent authorization decision path.
- Emit structured, tamper-evident audit events for every allow/deny...

title: Stream debug replay and cancellation-signal UX for Python SDK
preview: Problem: Developers building streaming chat integrations frequently hit hard-to-reproduce issues when streams are interrupted, events are reordered, or retries occur. Current patterns require application-level wiring for checkpointing, cancellation, and replay which leads to boilerplate and fragile integrations. Goa...
text: Problem: Developers building streaming chat integrations frequently hit hard-to-reproduce issues when streams are interrupted, events are reordered, or retries occur. Current patterns require application-level wiring for checkpointing, cancellation, and replay which leads to boilerplate and fragile integrations. Goal: Provide ergonomic, battery-included primitives in the Python SDK to (1) emit rich sideband telemetry and debug blobs for every streaming session, (2) expose a lightweight replay mode for local debugging and deterministic test harnesses, and (3) introduce a small cancellation/signal API usable from both sync and async code without forcing callback-heavy designs.

title: kmshub: Terraform orchestrator for KMS/HSM secrets failover in VPC/private deploys
preview: Deliver a reusable Terraform module (kmshub) that orchestrates KMS/HSM integrations and secrets management fallbacks for private (VPC) Redwood deployments. The module should: 1) provision KMS connectors (cloud KMS or on-prem HSM proxy) and Secrets Engine bindings (Secrets Manager or Vault) in a deterministic, audita...
text: Deliver a reusable Terraform module (kmshub) that orchestrates KMS/HSM integrations and secrets management fallbacks for private (VPC) Redwood deployments. The module should: 1) provision KMS connectors (cloud KMS or on-prem HSM proxy) and Secrets Engine bindings (Secrets Manager or Vault) in a deterministic, auditable way; 2) wire health checks and circuit-breaker policies so runtime can automatically failover to a safe secrets provider or cached keys if the primary KMS/HSM becomes unreachable; 3) include an automated upgrade path (minor/major) that preserves key material references and rotates provider bindings with minimal downtime; 4) produce drift-detection outputs and a remediation plan (tf plan-based alerting) for operators; 5) work in air-gapped and VPC-proxied environments, with optional sidecar proxy for hardware key brokers. This ticket scopes the module design, CI tests, a...

title: Hosted SSE client-hang mode diagnostics and staged remediation plan
preview: Multiple customers reported streaming requests (SSE) that emit a subset of tokens then appear to 'hang' without a final event or EOF. Clients either wait indefinitely or reconnect and observe duplicate/overlapping tokens. Issue surfaces across Hosted and several Dedicated tenants after the 2026-02-14 edge rollout. T...
text: Multiple customers reported streaming requests (SSE) that emit a subset of tokens then appear to 'hang' without a final event or EOF. Clients either wait indefinitely or reconnect and observe duplicate/overlapping tokens. Issue surfaces across Hosted and several Dedicated tenants after the 2026-02-14 edge rollout. This ticket contains triage notes, an interim mitigation, and a staged fix + rollout plan with QA and monitoring guidance.

title: Unify SDK retry semantics + streaming ergonomics and add changelog automation for v2 deprecations
preview: Background: v1 SDKs evolved independently and exhibit subtle differences in retry behaviour (default backoffs, idempotency assumptions) and streaming ergonomics (callback-heavy APIs, inconsistent cancellation/backpressure patterns). This has led to user confusion when migrating between languages, flaky integrations,...
text:
Background: v1 SDKs evolved independently and exhibit subtle differences in retry behaviour (default backoffs, idempotency assumptions) and streaming ergonomics (callback-heavy APIs, inconsistent cancellation/backpressure patterns). This has led to user confusion when migrating between languages, flaky integrations, and duplicated docs.

Goal: Define and implement a single, cross-language contract for retry semantics and streaming ergonomics in SDK v2, provide clear migration docs and language-specific examples (Python/TypeScript/Go), and build a small changelog automation tool that detects deprecated APIs across SDKs and generates a consolidated deprecation changelog entry for releases.

Scope:
- Design and implement the retry policy API surface across Python/TS/Go SDKs (RetryPolicy object / options, idempotency marker, standard backoff presets).
- Replace ad-hoc streaming callbacks ...

title: Guided POP routing with quantile-differential and link-safety score
preview: Problem statement: We need a region/POP (Point-of-Presence) assignment policy that is resilient to transient spikes that inflate p95 while preserving tail SLOs, and that can also rapidly avoid links showing degraded packet-loss or high retransmit rates. Existing approaches overreact to short-lived p95 excursions or ...
text:
Problem statement: We need a region/POP (Point-of-Presence) assignment policy that is resilient to transient spikes that inflate p95 while preserving tail SLOs, and that can also rapidly avoid links showing degraded packet-loss or high retransmit rates. Existing approaches overreact to short-lived p95 excursions or ignore cross-metric correlation.

Proposal summary: Implement a 'quantile-differential with link-safety' guider that computes a composite preference score per POP combining: (1) smoothed p50 and p95 (EWMA windowed), (2) the p95-p50 delta (as a sensitivity factor), and (3) a network link-safety penalty derived from loss/rtx/icmp-failure telemetry. The guider will provide ranked POP choices and a binary failover trigger when safety thresholds breach. It prioritizes stable low-latency POPs but gracefully shifts when tail-risk or network degradation is sustained.

Algorithm (hi...

title: Precision lane traffic controller for demand-driven FP8/INT8 dispatch
preview: Problem: Dequantization overhead and mismatched kernel precision choices are a dominant cost for many streaming/long-sequence workloads. Current heuristics select INT8 vs FP8 at model-load or route time, which misses opportunity to route on per-request latency/quality characteristics and to amortize dequant costs ac...
text:
Problem: Dequantization overhead and mismatched kernel precision choices are a dominant cost for many streaming/long-sequence workloads. Current heuristics select INT8 vs FP8 at model-load or route time, which misses opportunity to route on per-request latency/quality characteristics and to amortize dequant costs across microbatches.

Goal: Implement a Precision Lane Traffic Controller (PLTC) that dynamically admits requests into precision lanes (INT8 lane, FP8 lane, FP16 fallback) at runtime based on a short-term admission window. The controller should: (1) reduce per-token dequant overhead by grouping compatible requests, (2) avoid high tail latency by gating slow/misaligned requests to higher-precision lanes, and (3) provide telemetry for per-lane quality/latency tradeoffs.

What this ticket delivers: design, prototype, and a staged rollout of the admission controller and a lightwe...

title: Billing attestation: chain-of-custody, retention schedule, and least-privilege access matrix
preview: Create a repeatable attestation package for billing and metering evidence that satisfies SOC-style evidence requests and internal compliance checks. Deliverables include: a documented chain-of-custody for usage events, a retention schedule mapped to legal and commercial requirements, an explicit least-privilege acce...
text: Create a repeatable attestation package for billing and metering evidence that satisfies SOC-style evidence requests and internal compliance checks. Deliverables include: a documented chain-of-custody for usage events, a retention schedule mapped to legal and commercial requirements, an explicit least-privilege access matrix for services and people, and automated artifacts (signed snapshots, tamper-evident manifests) that can be exported to auditors.

title: Concilium: token-coalescer for quantized kernel alignment
preview: Implement a token-coalescing runtime layer (Concilium) that reorganizes prefills and microbatches to maximize reuse for INT8/FP8-friendly blocked kernels. The goal is to reduce dequantization overhead and improve kernel alignment for attention and KV prefill paths without changing model semantics. Concilium will: 1)...
text: Implement a token-coalescing runtime layer (Concilium) that reorganizes prefills and microbatches to maximize reuse for INT8/FP8-friendly blocked kernels. The goal is to reduce dequantization overhead and improve kernel alignment for attention and KV prefill paths without changing model semantics. Concilium will: 1) coalesce adjacent token slices across streams where safe, 2) introduce a headwise remap metadata layer so blocked kernels can operate on a coalesced memory layout, 3) provide lazy dequantization views to avoid materializing full FP tensors when not needed, and 4) coordinate prefill scheduling so that coalesced blocks are filled and decoded with minimal synchronization overhead.

title: Stratified prefetch sentinel with tiered denylist guards
preview: Problem: We need a safe, per-tenant and per-route mechanism that recommends adaptive prefetch (KV/prefix) and batching configurations to reduce per-token cost while preserving tail-latency SLOs. Existing policy runners produce global suggestions that either under-optimize low-volume tenants or risk quality regressio...
text: Problem: We need a safe, per-tenant and per-route mechanism that recommends adaptive prefetch (KV/prefix) and batching configurations to reduce per-token cost while preserving tail-latency SLOs. Existing policy runners produce global suggestions that either under-optimize low-volume tenants or risk quality regressions for high-priority customers. This ticket designs and implements a \"Sentinel\" layer that fences automated config changes with tiered denylist controls (deny, allow, soft-allow), per-tenant segmentation, and an offline simulation + dry-run pipeline before live apply.

title: Investigate and hotpatch edge-case latency regression affecting customer workloads across Hosted/Dedicated/Private
preview:
Summary:
A small subset (~2%) of long-session requests from two paying customers began exhibiting severe initial token latency (TTFT) regressions and reduced tokens/sec on 2026-03-09 after a rollout of runtime-1.25 -> runtime-1.26. The impact spans Hosted, Dedicated, and Private deployments but is most visible for: ...
text:
Summary:
A small subset (~2%) of long-session requests from two paying customers began exhibiting severe initial token latency (TTFT) regressions and reduced tokens/sec on 2026-03-09 after a rollout of runtime-1.25 -> runtime-1.26. The impact spans Hosted, Dedicated, and Private deployments but is most visible for: (a) long system prompts (>2k tokens), (b) models with KV-quant variants, and (c) routes using the Sherpa scheduler hint for lower-cost paths.

Observed behavior:
- TTFT increases from median 180ms to 1.2s for affected requests; tokens/sec drops by ~60% in steady-state for those sessions.
- Regression correlates with requests that trigger cross-worker KV prefetch and also hit a recently-introduced Sherpa hint path that defers kernel selection for up to 100ms while probing variant warm state.
- Customer workload traces (see dashboards) show a high rate of repeated KV cache mi...

title: KV cache eviction regression & poison-susceptibility follow-up
preview: Summary: During a production capacity run on 2026-02-28 we observed repeated latency spikes and degraded throughput across several hosted clusters. Root cause analysis identified a compound failure mode in the KV/prefix cache: an eviction heuristic regression combined with an edge-case in cache key normalization all...
text:
Summary: During a production capacity run on 2026-02-28 we observed repeated latency spikes and degraded throughput across several hosted clusters. Root cause analysis identified a compound failure mode in the KV/prefix cache: an eviction heuristic regression combined with an edge-case in cache key normalization allowed stale/duplicated entries to remain referenced, causing memory growth and periodic cache poisoning (incorrect KV lookup results for hot prefixes). This ticket tracks follow-ups and action items from the postmortem aiming to fully remediate the regression, add guardrails, and close monitoring gaps.

What happened: A change in the eviction score calculation (PR #1984) altered LRU aging for small shard segments. Under typical workloads this increased cache churn, but under a specific prefix distribution we saw double-insert races where identical logical keys were stored wi...

title: Workload-temporal coalescer: heuristics, profile-driven parameters, and safe apply
preview: Goal: implement a workload-temporal coalescer that recommends continuous batching parameters and safe config apply/rollback policies based on multi-dimensional workload profiles. This ticket covers the profiling pipeline, heuristic parameterization (window sizes, hold thresholds, micro-coalescing probabilities), the...
text: Goal: implement a workload-temporal coalescer that recommends continuous batching parameters and safe config apply/rollback policies based on multi-dimensional workload profiles. This ticket covers the profiling pipeline, heuristic parameterization (window sizes, hold thresholds, micro-coalescing probabilities), the shadow-apply + canary rollout strategy, and automated rollback triggers.

title: Go SDK: schema-first decoder patterns and streaming ergonomics
preview: Goal: Design and implement a schema-first decoder set for the Go SDK that provides strong compile-time structs, ergonomic generics-based helpers for common patterns, and streaming-friendly decoders that interoperate with the SDK's retry/backpressure model. This ticket covers design, a prototype library, integration ...
text: Goal: Design and implement a schema-first decoder set for the Go SDK that provides strong compile-time structs, ergonomic generics-based helpers for common patterns, and streaming-friendly decoders that interoperate with the SDK's retry/backpressure model. This ticket covers design, a prototype library, integration tests, and an example app demonstrating structured streaming + resumable retries.

title: Lane escrow for throughput handover and quota reconciliation
preview: Introduce a tenant lane escrow mechanism that reserves short-duration throughput credits, enables graceful handover between autoscaler modes, and provides an auditable quota reconciliation ledger to mitigate noisy neighbor effects and enforce throughput guarantees on Dedicated capacity.
text: Introduce a tenant lane escrow mechanism that reserves short-duration throughput credits, enables graceful handover between autoscaler modes, and provides an auditable quota reconciliation ledger to mitigate noisy neighbor effects and enforce throughput guarantees on Dedicated capacity.

title: Routing decision audit trail and per-hop telemetry enhancements
preview: Scope: Add machine-readable decision logs and sampled distributed traces for the smart routing layer that performs region selection, tier routing, and fallback to model variants. Provide per-hop metrics (latency, queue depth, token count) emitted at each routing hop (edge proxy -> router -> model-host) and correlate...
text: Scope: Add machine-readable decision logs and sampled distributed traces for the smart routing layer that performs region selection, tier routing, and fallback to model variants. Provide per-hop metrics (latency, queue depth, token count) emitted at each routing hop (edge proxy -> router -> model-host) and correlate them by a trace_id and decision_id. Deliverables: 1) decision_log schema and storage plan (low-cost cold storage + short-term hot index), 2) cross-service trace propagation and instrumentation, 3) per-hop metrics with aggregation keys for route_policy, model_variant, region, tier, and customer_tier, 4) dashboards and alerting for anomalous routing patterns, and 5) sampling & redaction policy to maintain privacy and SLOs. Non-goal: full request payload retention; only metadata and short decision fingerprints retained per policy.

title: Separate policy gatekeeper and async lease pool to reduce routing hotpath contention
preview: Large portions of the smart routing hotpath still execute synchronous policy checks and lease handling under a global mutex. This ticket extracts a lightweight policy gatekeeper (read-only decision cache + observer pipeline) and an asynchronous lease pool (per-region evicting lease queue) to remove contention, reduc...
text: Large portions of the smart routing hotpath still execute synchronous policy checks and lease handling under a global mutex. This ticket extracts a lightweight policy gatekeeper (read-only decision cache + observer pipeline) and an asynchronous lease pool (per-region evicting lease queue) to remove contention, reduce p99 tail latency, and make fallback decisions non-blocking.

title: Contingency choreography for active-passive divergence and staged resynchronization
preview: Objective: Create and validate an operational choreography for active-passive regional divergence windows that ensures safe partial promotions, deterministic KV reconciliation, and SLO-gated rollback. Scope: multi-region L2 services (inference gateway + tokenstore), KV snapshot orchestration, circuit-breaker fencing...
text: Objective: Create and validate an operational choreography for active-passive regional divergence windows that ensures safe partial promotions, deterministic KV reconciliation, and SLO-gated rollback. Scope: multi-region L2 services (inference gateway + tokenstore), KV snapshot orchestration, circuit-breaker fencing, staged traffic siphon, and end-to-end restore verification. Success criteria: (1) ability to perform a staged promotion from passive -> active with <5% request drop and median p95 latency regression <20% vs baseline in canary slice; (2) automated snapshot checksum compare and amber/gold/green reconciliation gates pass without manual data patching; (3) documented rollback path and automated fence (circuit-breaker) that prevents split-brain for 60m; (4) drill runbook exercised twice with postmortem and checklist ticked. Out-of-scope: full cold-start recovery from expired sn...

title: Schematic Stability Guardian: Stream-safe constrained-decoder + graceful schema-fallback test harness
preview: Problem: Streaming model outputs occasionally violate strict schemas in live traffic (partial JSON fragments, trailing tokens, or tool-call markers inserted mid-object). Current validators either fail the request (causing user-visible errors) or run expensive post-processing that increases latency. We need a test ha...
text:
Problem: Streaming model outputs occasionally violate strict schemas in live traffic (partial JSON fragments, trailing tokens, or tool-call markers inserted mid-object). Current validators either fail the request (causing user-visible errors) or run expensive post-processing that increases latency. We need a test harness and decoder shim that validates constrained decoding behavior under streaming conditions and exercises graceful fallback logic used by the platform when strict schema adherence cannot be asserted mid-stream.

Goal: Build a reproducible test harness (unit + integration + synthetic traffic) and an engineering prototype 'Schematic Stability Guardian' - a stream-safe constrained-decoder adapter that: (1) enforces constrained sampling where possible, (2) detects and classifies schema violations in-flight, and (3) triggers deterministic fallback behaviors (best-effort repai...

title: Automated Health Gate: telemetry-driven circuit thresholds for Private VPC upgrades
preview: Purpose: Add an automated, telemetry-driven health gate to our Terraform deployment pipeline for Private/VPC upgrades. The gate evaluates short-window SLOs (latency, error-rate, saturation, token throughput) and enforces an automated rollback or traffic quarantine when degradation exceeds configured thresholds. This...
text:
Purpose: Add an automated, telemetry-driven health gate to our Terraform deployment pipeline for Private/VPC upgrades. The gate evaluates short-window SLOs (latency, error-rate, saturation, token throughput) and enforces an automated rollback or traffic quarantine when degradation exceeds configured thresholds. This is an infra-level safety harness to make upgrades (runtime, kernel, model variant changes) across private clusters predictable and reversible without manual intervention.

Scope: Build a reusable Terraform module (redwood/vpc-health-gate) and integration patterns for: (1) pre-upgrade probes and dry-run checks, (2) continuous post-deploy evaluation windows (1m/5m/15m), (3) policy-driven automated rollback or traffic quarantining, (4) observability dashboard and runbook links in PRs, and (5) integration with Dedicated/Private routing policies for graceful fallback to compati...

title: Staged heterogeneous GPU dependency upgrade with runtime ABI adapters and graceful fallbacks
preview: Background: Our GPU-serving fleet runs a mix of CUDA versions, Triton builds, and multiple CUTLASS profiles across cloud and on-prem nodes. Past upgrades caused a spike in cold-start latency and two incidents where symbol resolution failures triggered partial node evictions. Goal: Implement a staged upgrade orchestr...
text: Background: Our GPU-serving fleet runs a mix of CUDA versions, Triton builds, and multiple CUTLASS profiles across cloud and on-prem nodes. Past upgrades caused a spike in cold-start latency and two incidents where symbol resolution failures triggered partial node evictions. Goal: Implement a staged upgrade orchestration that (1) ships lightweight ABI adapter shims instead of inplace library swaps, (2) provides runtime probing and per-node capability stenciling, (3) exposes an operator-controlled canary + automated rollback policy, and (4) adds end-to-end telemetry and unit/regression tests to prevent regressions. Scope: runtime loader changes, new signed shim artifacts, CI performance benchmarks, canary pipeline in deployer, and updated rollout docs. Acceptance criteria: canary rollout to 5% of prod fleet without >5% increase in tail latency, zero symbol resolution errors in canary, ...

title: VPC Observability Terraform module: Prometheus/Grafana/OTel + log forwarder, upgrade & healthcheck plan
preview: Scope: Build a reusable Terraform module for VPC/private deployments that provisions a minimal observability stack opinionated for Redwood Private customers: Prometheus (federation-compatible), Grafana (provisioned dashboards + org config), OpenTelemetry Collector (receiver/processor/exporter pipeline), and a log-fo...
text:
Scope: Build a reusable Terraform module for VPC/private deployments that provisions a minimal observability stack opinionated for Redwood Private customers: Prometheus (federation-compatible), Grafana (provisioned dashboards + org config), OpenTelemetry Collector (receiver/processor/exporter pipeline), and a log-forwarder (Vector/Fluent Bit) that reliably ships container logs to our central logging tier or customer-managed endpoints.

Goals:
- Single Terraform module with clear inputs/outputs so tenants can opt in to managed observability when deploying Private.
- Support upgrade paths for collector, Prometheus, and Grafana without data loss or long downtime.
- Built-in healthchecks and self-healing signals (K8s probes, Prometheus rules, Grafana datasource tests) surfaced in Console.
- Small TF surface for platform teams: sensible defaults + override hooks.

Constraints & decisions:
...

title: Temporal reservoir batcher for skewed turn lengths and aggressive KV compaction
preview:
Problem statement:
Many production workloads contain a heavy skew between short prompts (1-2 turns) and long multi-turn dialogues. The current continuous-batching scheduler aggregates by arrival time and length buckets, but we observe repeated KV fragmentation and suboptimal GPU slab utilization when a small number ...
text:
Problem statement:
Many production workloads contain a heavy skew between short prompts (1-2 turns) and long multi-turn dialogues. The current continuous-batching scheduler aggregates by arrival time and length buckets, but we observe repeated KV fragmentation and suboptimal GPU slab utilization when a small number of long requests intermingle with high-volume short requests. This leads to transient OOMs, higher p50/p95 latency for long responses, and degraded token-per-dollar efficiency.

Proposal summary:
Introduce a Temporal Reservoir Batcher (TRB) that maintains short-term reservoirs per-GPU weighted by observed turn-length histograms and recent KV residency. The TRB will: 1) allocate a soft-reservation of KV slots for long-turn pipelines (reservoir windows) based on tail-frequency forecasts; 2) perform opportunistic prefetching with a stride-aware policy to pull KV shards for imm...

title: Tool parameter spec style guide and runtime recovery patterns
preview: Purpose: define a single, pragmatic JSON parameter specification and a set of runtime compatibility + recovery behaviors for function/tool calling. Scope: param schema style (shape conventions, primitive coercions, optional vs required), three validation modes (strict/lenient/coerce), canonical error payloads and ma...
text: Purpose: define a single, pragmatic JSON parameter specification and a set of runtime compatibility + recovery behaviors for function/tool calling. Scope: param schema style (shape conventions, primitive coercions, optional vs required), three validation modes (strict/lenient/coerce), canonical error payloads and mappings, and a lightweight shim strategy for older clients and model variants. Non-goals: full JSON Schema v2020 implementation and client-side SDK changes beyond minimal helpers.

title: Route consistency window during peering promote causes transient resolver-binding drift
preview: Summary: During a peering promote (when a secondary peering session is promoted to primary to balance load or recover capacity), some Dedicated tenant routes can appear to be consistent at the control plane level while dataplane bindings (egress resolver affinity and next-hop for SNAT) lag by a small window (1-30s)....
text: Summary: During a peering promote (when a secondary peering session is promoted to primary to balance load or recover capacity), some Dedicated tenant routes can appear to be consistent at the control plane level while dataplane bindings (egress resolver affinity and next-hop for SNAT) lag by a small window (1-30s). The lag can cause tenant traffic to be forwarded to an egress path that no longer has the correct resolver mapping, producing transient DNS misresolution and connection resets for long-lived client sessions. Several customer escalations reported 5-30s spikes of 500-level proxy errors and DNS NXDOMAINs coincident with peering promotions during scheduled maintenance or autoscaler-driven reparent events.

title: Token fingerprinting harness for per-model throughput + p95 regression detection
preview: Create and maintain a reproducible microbenchmark harness that fingerprints tokens/sec and p95 latency for our core served model variants (float32, int8/quantized, and compiled-kernel builds). This harness will be the canonical source-of-truth for per-model throughput baselines used by CI, rollout decisions, and bil...
text: Create and maintain a reproducible microbenchmark harness that fingerprints tokens/sec and p95 latency for our core served model variants (float32, int8/quantized, and compiled-kernel builds). This harness will be the canonical source-of-truth for per-model throughput baselines used by CI, rollout decisions, and billing guidance. It must exercise streaming & non-streaming paths, warm KV cache behavior, and sequence-length sensitivity.

title: Streaming token ingress lane: low-latency preprocessing and hot-prefix cache
preview: Problem: The prefill/prompt ingestion stage is a measurable hotspot for short-prompt workloads: per-request tokenization, normalization, and prefix handling add ~8–30ms tail latency in our hosted path and amplify CPU pressure on Dedicated nodes. This is particularly visible for streaming requests and small-batch dec...
text:
Problem: The prefill/prompt ingestion stage is a measurable hotspot for short-prompt workloads: per-request tokenization, normalization, and prefix handling add ~8–30ms tail latency in our hosted path and amplify CPU pressure on Dedicated nodes. This is particularly visible for streaming requests and small-batch decode lanes where decode-side compute is tiny compared to prefill overhead.

Goal: Reduce per-request prefill overhead by 40–60% for common short prompts (<=64 tokens) while preserving correctness for complex inputs and ensuring CPU usage scales safely under bursty traffic. Secondary goals: reduce p50/p95 of prompt preprocessing, reduce cold-start tokenization cost, and provide knobs to trade CPU vs. memory for Dedicated customers.

Proposed approach: Implement a dedicated "streaming token ingress lane" in the runtime that combines three techniques: (1) hot-prefix microcache ...

title: Tokenwise requant budgeter and kernel provenance harness for dovetailed attention scheduling
preview: Problem: Mixed-precision pipelines (INT8/FP8) introduce per-token dequant/requant overhead that is disproportionately paid during prefill and early decode windows, increasing P95 latency and token cost for short-turn conversations. Current kernel selection lacks fine-grained token-aware budgeting and provenance tele...
text:
Problem: Mixed-precision pipelines (INT8/FP8) introduce per-token dequant/requant overhead that is disproportionately paid during prefill and early decode windows, increasing P95 latency and token cost for short-turn conversations. Current kernel selection lacks fine-grained token-aware budgeting and provenance telemetry, making it hard to decide when to incur a dequant vs. pick a slower FP32/FP16 kernel.

Proposal: Implement a "tokenwise requant budgeter" in the runtime scheduler that tracks a sliding budget across the active token window and decides, per token/head-group, whether to route to an INT8 fastpath with a requantization at the epilogue or to a safer FP8/FP16 kernel. Complement this with a "kernel provenance harness": lightweight telemetry attached to each kernel decision that records the features used to decide the path (sequence length, kv-hit ratio, headwise entropy, bat...

title: Plan-driven runtime rollouts: Terraform-gated staged migration with preflight priming and reversible snapshot restore
preview: Motivation: We need a reproducible, plan-driven way to migrate private (VPC) data-plane runtimes with zero-downtime guarantees and a safe, deterministic rollback path. This ticket defines a playbook + Terraform gating mechanism that uses the plan output (diff), automated preflight validation, lightweight priming pro...
text:
Motivation: We need a reproducible, plan-driven way to migrate private (VPC) data-plane runtimes with zero-downtime guarantees and a safe, deterministic rollback path. This ticket defines a playbook + Terraform gating mechanism that uses the plan output (diff), automated preflight validation, lightweight priming probes, and a reversible snapshot workflow for KV/state to enable fast, low-risk rollbacks.

Scope:
- Add a terraform-plan gate step that annotates planned resource changes and computes a risk score (instance churn, network changes, storage moves).
- Implement a preflight controller that runs in the target VPC slice: applies ephemeral terraform in a staging namespace to create a mirrored runtime slice (no public traffic).
- Run the following validations against the mirrored slice: Terraform diff assertion, synthetic traffic replay (rho-based workload sampled from recent produc...

title: Short-term amplification zones and weighted overdraft invoicing for Dedicated fleets
preview: Problem: Customers on Dedicated fleets occasionally need short, high-throughput windows (e.g., product launches) that exceed committed throughput. Today we either reject requests, throttle aggressively, or let requests succeed and then issue blunt overage line items that are hard to explain. Goal: implement 'amplifi...
text: Problem: Customers on Dedicated fleets occasionally need short, high-throughput windows (e.g., product launches) that exceed committed throughput. Today we either reject requests, throttle aggressively, or let requests succeed and then issue blunt overage line items that are hard to explain. Goal: implement 'amplification zones' — controlled, temporary capacity lifts that are 1) meterable at sub-minute granularity, 2) governed by per-tenant weighting and caps, and 3) produce a transparent, prorated overdraft invoice line (weighted overdraft invoicing) instead of opaque overage charges. This ticket covers design and implementation of the allocation algorithm, autoscaling hooks, telemetry, UI flags, and the billing attribution surface for invoices.

title: Isolate transitive crypto deps with runtime policy gate and link-time hiding
preview: Goal: eliminate accidental exposure and symbol collisions from transitive cryptographic dependencies across the inference runtime and control plane services. We will introduce a runtime policy gate enforced at build/link time (symbol hiding + consumption whitelist) and a CI binary-scan to detect exported crypto symb...
text: Goal: eliminate accidental exposure and symbol collisions from transitive cryptographic dependencies across the inference runtime and control plane services. We will introduce a runtime policy gate enforced at build/link time (symbol hiding + consumption whitelist) and a CI binary-scan to detect exported crypto symbols. This reduces attack surface, prevents mixed-OpenSSL ABI issues, and provides a safe upgrade path for OpenSSL/JW* libraries used transitively by third-party dependencies.

title: session-pool-ballooning-and-prefix-cache-pointer-race-runtime-fix
preview: Summary: Multiple customers (Hosted burst pools and a Private deployment for a regulated customer) reported sudden GPU OOM spikes followed by degraded throughput. Investigation shows a previously unobserved race between session pool growth (we've named it 'ballooning') and the prefix-store tombstone sweep which caus...
text:
Summary: Multiple customers (Hosted burst pools and a Private deployment for a regulated customer) reported sudden GPU OOM spikes followed by degraded throughput. Investigation shows a previously unobserved race between session pool growth (we've named it 'ballooning') and the prefix-store tombstone sweep which caused stale pointer chains to accumulate in the prefix cache metadata, preventing GC and causing page saturation on GPU arenas. The failure surface included increased allocator retention, long-lived session objects pinned by expired prefix entries, and cascading OOMs under bursty traffic patterns.

Impact: Incident affected three customer environments intermittently over the past 36 hours: two hosted burst pools (short latency spikes then OOMs) and one private cluster where a single tenant experienced sustained OOMs and service degradation for ~45 minutes during peak. Traffic ...

title: Cost Sentinel: Continuous Workload Profiling and Safe Batch Parameter Synthesizer
preview: Deliver a service component (Cost Sentinel) that continuously profiles production request streams to synthesize safe batching parameters and caching heuristics. The synthesizer will propose incremental config updates that can be applied automatically under conservative safety checks, and provide an automatic rollbac...
text: Deliver a service component (Cost Sentinel) that continuously profiles production request streams to synthesize safe batching parameters and caching heuristics. The synthesizer will propose incremental config updates that can be applied automatically under conservative safety checks, and provide an automatic rollback path when SLOs regress or error rates increase. This ticket scopes the initial engine, telemetry contracts, canary rollout plan, and integration points with the Console rollout APIs and the autoscaling subsystem.

title: KV hit allocator and TTL continuous-deployment playbook
preview:
Goal: build a simulator + recommendation engine and a staged-apply playbook that can safely propose per-prefix TTLs, cache-key transforms, and batching-friendly key shaping to reduce per-token inference cost while respecting latency SLOs and rollback constraints.

Scope: offline workload ingest (trace sampler), hit-...
text:
Goal: build a simulator + recommendation engine and a staged-apply playbook that can safely propose per-prefix TTLs, cache-key transforms, and batching-friendly key shaping to reduce per-token inference cost while respecting latency SLOs and rollback constraints.

Scope: offline workload ingest (trace sampler), hit-allocation model (per-prefix hit-rate forecasting with uncertainty), TTL/policy optimizer (cost vs latency trade-off curve), recommendation output (TTL, key rules, expected cost delta, confidence band), and a staged apply/rollback harness integrated with the existing deployment orchestrator and Console.

Model approach: start with a Bayesian hit-allocation model (beta-binomial smoothing for low-traffic prefixes) complemented by a Poisson arrival model for per-prefix request rates. Use hierarchical priors to borrow strength across prefixes with similar token length and route...

title: Reservation deficit detector and graded throttling ramp for noisy tenants
preview: Summary: After the 2026-02-11 capacity incident we observed recurring evictions and elevated P99 latencies caused by tenants that rapidly consumed burst tokens and drained node reservations. This ticket implements a lightweight "reservation deficit" detector that continuously estimates when a tenant's effective usag...
text: Summary: After the 2026-02-11 capacity incident we observed recurring evictions and elevated P99 latencies caused by tenants that rapidly consumed burst tokens and drained node reservations. This ticket implements a lightweight "reservation deficit" detector that continuously estimates when a tenant's effective usage exceeds its reservation over a short rolling window and applies a graded throttling ramp instead of immediate hard ejection. Goals: reduce noisy-neighbor-driven evictions, preserve tail latency for stable tenants, and provide transparent signal for operators to decide reservation reconciliation or customer outreach.

title: SLO Arbitration Fabric: proportional soft-reservations & transient KV slab leasing for continuous batching
preview: Problem: The current continuous batching system uses hard preemption heuristics that either over-evict KV cache (hurting long-context workloads) or favor throughput-heavy tenants at the expense of latency SLOs for small interactive tenants. Goals: 1) Implement an SLO Arbitration Fabric (SAF) that enforces per-tenant...
text: Problem: The current continuous batching system uses hard preemption heuristics that either over-evict KV cache (hurting long-context workloads) or favor throughput-heavy tenants at the expense of latency SLOs for small interactive tenants. Goals: 1) Implement an SLO Arbitration Fabric (SAF) that enforces per-tenant latency budgets while preserving aggregate GPU utilization; 2) Introduce proportional soft-reservations for batch slots and transient KV slab leases to avoid hard eviction storms; 3) Provide a predictable degradation path that trades small throughput drops for latency guarantees; 4) Integrate monitoring, simulation, and rollout plan to validate safety before full rollout.

title: Sinuous regional routing policy: composite latency + capacity steering with safe fallbacks
preview: Design and implement a new multi-region request steering policy (codename: 'sinuous') that combines latency-based routing, geo pinning for affinity, and capacity-aware steering into a single compact decision function. The goal is to improve SLO adherence for 99th pct latency during cross-region incidents while minim...
text: Design and implement a new multi-region request steering policy (codename: 'sinuous') that combines latency-based routing, geo pinning for affinity, and capacity-aware steering into a single compact decision function. The goal is to improve SLO adherence for 99th pct latency during cross-region incidents while minimizing unnecessary cross-region egress and avoiding capacity hot-spots. The policy must integrate circuit breaker signals and graded load-shedding tiers for graceful degradation.
